text:
Eagled-eyed viewers spotted a major mistake during a key moment of the Opening Ceremony on Saturday morning in Paris, which has received mixed reviews from around the world.

Eagle-eyed viewers have been left shocked when they watched the Olympic flag hoisted upside down during an important part of the Opening Ceremony.

After the parade of 184 nations on boats along Paris' River Seine and spectacular performances, athletes from around the world gathered to the grandstand-style seating near the Eiffel Tower to watch the rest of the proceedings on Friday night.

The Olympic flag was carried by what was understood to be a Gendarmerie officer who rode a silver metal horse along the river to represent the "spirit" of the event.

French President Emmanuel Macron later declared the games as officially open and the neatly folded Olympic flag was taken to officials to raise it on the flagpole.

However, it had not been noticed the flag was clipped on upside down.

Viewers immediately spotted the major blunder.

"Those who managed to stay awake during the Opening Ceremonies were treated to the Olympic flag being flown upside down," one social media user wrote.

"Oh no. They hung the Olympic flag upside down," a second person spotted.

The big spectacle was washed out by the weather, the display was cringe & to top it all off the Olympic flag was hoisted upside down at the zenith of it allThis event is like a microcosm of modern France, an utter shamblespic.twitter.com/eZeyCrIXRQ

Another joked: "If you're having a bad day at work, it could be worse. You could be the flag team that hoisted the Olympic flag upside down at the opening ceremony."

A fourth social media described the event as a "shambles".

"The big spectacle was washed out by the weather, the display was cringe & to top it all off the Olympic flag was hoisted upside down at the zenith of it all," they wrote.

The flag appeared to have been folded the whole time, so the officials were unaware.

But that was not the only issue.

The Opening Ceremony kicked off under dark skies, with the threat of wet weather eventually turning into a nightmare as rain fell on spectators and athletes.

France had decided it would host the event outside a stadium for the first time, which showcased the city of Paris and its famous landmarks.

However, some viewers struggled to watch it on television.

"The sound from this opening ceremony is terrible. The rain was louder than any of the music at one point," one social media user wrote.

"This was the most pathetic opening ceremony of any Olympics I have ever witnessed just stupid and God showed his displeasure by pouring rain," a viewer said.

The City of Light sparkles in an unforgettable Opening Ceremony.Read more âž¡ï¸https://t.co/ULKPnCizgS#AllezAUS|@Sportscraft|#VolleyAustralia

Others loved seeing it changed up.

"Really different, a bit bonkers, the rain, all the boats, the painting flame thing to start, even the 90s euro dance music, the water horse, Rafa!, Celine, and the balloon flame. Well done France. We won't forget this opening ceremony for sure," one person said.

"Macron was not kidding. This opening ceremony was incredible. It was a total sensory overload, even with it pouring down rain," another added.

While it was a unique experience for all involved, athletes on boats and spectators lining the River Seine were left to scramble for their umbrellas and ponchos.

Most of the 80 Australian competitors decided to brave the rain and were left drenched from head to toe before arriving for the formalities at the Eiffel Tower.

Rain fell for the majority of the Opening Ceremony.

title: Paris Games Opening Ceremony viewers left shocked as Olympic flag is hoisted upside down during big moment | Sky News Australia
description: Eagle-eyed viewers have been left shocked when they watched the Olympic flag hoisted upside down during an important part of the Opening Ceremony.
